source.sourcePath: raw/sources/DHF (인허가)/07_(CYAN) GT1717C, GT1717CP/91_인증 - 국내/02. G series + CYAN/3. 보완(제출)★/★보완제출문서/03 기술문서 심사에 관한 자료/03 제품의 성능 및 안전을 확인하기 위한 자료 (Rev)/01 전기·기계적 안전에 관한 자료/2.2 [GT1717C] IEC60601-1 Test report 1 (ed.3.2 CB).pdf

document.title: IEC 60601-1 Test Report, Report No. ACTS-2025-SM-261
document.documentType: CB Scheme medical electrical equipment safety test report with IEC 60601-1-6 usability annex
document.revisionOrVersion: TRF No. IEC60601_1U; Annex A TRF No. IEC60601_1_6L; referenced manufacturer documents Rev.0

sourceSummary.oneLine: Chunk covers IEC 60601-1 safety tables, risk management, leakage/current, mechanical, temperature, software IEC 62304, and IEC 60601-1-6 usability evidence for GT1717C.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports regulatory technical-file evidence for electrical/mechanical safety, risk management, software lifecycle, usability engineering, and CB Scheme conformity.
keyPoints: Mechanical strength, instability, protective earth, leakage current, temperature, cleaning/disinfection, and single-fault results are reported as passing where applicable.
Risk management references A-RMP-CYN Rev.0 and A-RMR-CYN Rev.0 against ISO 14971 clauses 4.1 through 9.
Software is evaluated against IEC 62304:2006+AMD1:2015 and classified as safety class B.
Annex A documents IEC 60601-1-6 usability evaluation issued 2025-11-11 by ACTS Co., Ltd.

entities.name: ACTS Co., Ltd.
entities.evidence: Annex A identifies ACTS Co., Ltd. as the testing laboratory preparing the report and CB Testing Laboratory.

entities.name: Habyz Co., Ltd.
entities.evidence: Annex A applicant name is Habyz Co., Ltd., with address in Yongin-si, Gyeonggi-do, Republic of Korea.

entities.name: GT1717C
entities.evidence: Temperature table 11.1.1 lists model no. GT1717C.

entities.name: Phihong Technology Co., Ltd.
entities.evidence: Critical components table lists POE injector manufacturer Phihong Technology Co., Ltd., model POE29U-1AT(PL).

entities.name: YoonCheol Kim
entities.evidence: Annex A responsible testing laboratory page lists tested by YoonCheol Kim, Project Handler.

entities.name: JaeYoung Lee
entities.evidence: Annex A responsible testing laboratory page lists approved by JaeYoung Lee, Project Reviewer.

concepts.name: Risk management file conformity
concepts.evidence: RM results table maps ISO 14971 clauses 4.1 through 9 to A-RMP-CYN Rev.0 and A-RMR-CYN Rev.0 with verdict P.

concepts.name: Essential performance: image acquire
concepts.evidence: Essential performance table lists 'Image acquire' and notes essential performance confirmed after each test with no functional problems.

concepts.name: Software safety class B
concepts.evidence: IEC 62304 attachment states software system classified as class B with reference to Software Validation Report A-SVR-CYN Rev.0.

concepts.name: No SOUP used
concepts.evidence: IEC 62304 clauses 5.3.3, 5.3.4, and 8.1.2 state no SOUP used or identified.

concepts.name: Not an ME system
concepts.evidence: Clause 16 ME Systems entries repeatedly state 'Not a ME system' and related requirements are N/A.

concepts.name: EMC outside report scope
concepts.evidence: Clause 17 states electromagnetic compatibility was not evaluated in this report and should refer to a separate EMC test report.

evidence.claim: GT1717C mechanical strength testing passed applicable push, impact, and portable drop tests without damage or hazard.
evidence.evidenceText: Table 15.3: Push Test 250 N ±10 N for 5 s, Impact Test steel ball 50 mm/500 g from 1.3 m, Drop Test 5 cm; observed results 'No damage. No hazard.'

evidence.claim: Risk management documentation was reviewed against ISO 14971 and marked pass for the general process through risk management review.
evidence.evidenceText: RM Results Table lists Risk Management Plan A-RMP-CYN Rev.0 and Risk Management Report A-RMR-CYN Rev.0 for ISO 14971 clauses 4.1, 4.2, 4.3, 4.4, 4.5, 5.1-5.5, 6, 7.1-7.6, 8, and 9 with verdict P.

evidence.claim: The report identifies 'Image acquire' as the essential performance function.
evidence.evidenceText: Essential Performance table lists 'Image acquire' with Risk Management Report Doc. No. A-TCF-10-104 Rev.0 and note that essential performance was confirmed after each test with no functional problems.

evidence.claim: Power input testing covered AC mains from 90 V to 264 V at 50/60 Hz and a 56 V d.c. condition.
evidence.evidenceText: Power Input table lists normal load conditions at 90, 100, 220, 240, and 264 V with 50/60 Hz; 56 V d.c. condition measured 0.261 A and 14.616 W.

evidence.claim: Accessible parts were determined by visual and jointed test finger methods.
evidence.evidenceText: Determination of Accessible Parts table lists cable terminal, carbon plate, external metal enclosure, and power switch as accessible parts by visual and jointed test finger.

evidence.claim: Markings were readable under the tested illumination conditions and durable after solvent rub testing.
evidence.evidenceText: Legibility table reports outside markings, controls/instruments, safety signs, and symbols clearly readable at 100 and 1,500 lx; durability table reports pressure-sensitive printed label with no loosening/curling and clearly readable after 15 s water, ethanol 96%, and isopropyl alcohol rubs.

evidence.claim: Protective earth connection impedance complied with applicable limits.
evidence.evidenceText: Table 8.6.4 measured 18 mΩ versus 100 mΩ allowed between appliance inlet earth pin and protectively earthed part, and 47 mΩ versus 200 mΩ allowed through detachable power supply cord.

evidence.claim: Earth leakage and touch current testing reported measured values below stated maximum allowed values.
evidence.evidenceText: Leakage current table states earth leakage maximum allowed 5 mA NC and 10 mA SFC; measured values include 45/46 µA NC and up to 96/96 µA SFC. Touch current maximum allowed 100 µA NC and 500 µA SFC; listed measured touch-current values are below these limits.

evidence.claim: Dielectric strength testing for MOOP insulation areas E and F used 3,000 V and reported no breakdown.
evidence.evidenceText: Table 8.8.3 lists insulation areas E and F as 2 MOOP, reference voltage 240, working voltage 340, AC test voltage 3,000 V, dielectric breakdown after 1 minute: No.

evidence.claim: Critical component list includes certified power cords, POE injector, PCB, and metal enclosure information.
evidence.evidenceText: Critical components table lists power supply cord sets for Europe, US, and Korea; POE injector Phihong POE29U-1AT(PL) with IEC 62368-1:2018 and UL Solutions Demko mark; PCB UL 796; metal enclosure tested in equipment.

evidence.claim: Instability overbalance testing passed at 10° in both transport and non-transport conditions.
evidence.evidenceText: Tables 9.4.2.1 and 9.4.2.2 list Product tested at 10° with remark 'Not overbalance' and verdict P.

evidence.claim: Grip/handling test passed with a 150 N force applied for 1 minute.
evidence.evidenceText: Table 9.4.4 reports test force 150 N, test time 1 min, applied to optional handle uniformly 7 cm, with result 'No damage, No hazard.'

evidence.claim: Temperature testing on GT1717C remained within listed maximum allowable temperatures.
evidence.evidenceText: Table 11.1.1 for GT1717C lists thermocouple locations with allowable limits such as 105 °C for PCB locations, 56 °C cable terminal, 51 °C carbon plate/external metal enclosure, and measured corrected temperatures below listed limits.

evidence.claim: Cleaning and disinfection evaluation used 70% IPA on a fabric or soft cloth and reported no damage or safety hazard.
evidence.evidenceText: Table 11.6.1 states clean detector with 70% IPA when contaminated, do not pour or spray directly, use fabric or soft cloth moistened with IPA; part under test unit; remarks 'No damage, No safety hazard.'

evidence.claim: Single-fault electrical conditions did not create a hazardous situation for the listed conductor interruptions.
evidence.evidenceText: Table 13.2 lists open protective earth conductor, interruption of one supply conductor, and interruption of one power-carrying conductor in separate enclosure; results did not exceed leakage current limits and hazardous situation marked No.

evidence.claim: Software was evaluated to IEC 62304:2006+AMD1:2015 and classified as safety class B.
evidence.evidenceText: Attachment Software IEC 62304 states manufacturer assigns software safety class; class B criterion row says 'Classified as class B' with verdict P and references Software Validation Report A-SVR-CYN Rev.0.

evidence.claim: Software development planning references Software Development Plan A-SDP-CYN Rev.0 and includes lifecycle, traceability, configuration/change management, and problem resolution.
evidence.evidenceText: IEC 62304 clause 5.1.1 references Software Development Plan Document No. A-SDP-CYN Rev.0; clauses list lifecycle fully identified, traceability, software configuration/change management, and software problem resolution with verdict P.

evidence.claim: Software requirements include security requirements and regulatory requirements, while IT-network requirements are not applicable because the device is not intended to connect to an IT network.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 5.2.2 lists security requirements and regulatory requirements with verdict P; IT-network aspects row states 'Not intended to be connected to IT-network' and N/A.

evidence.claim: Software release records include verification completion, residual anomalies evaluation, released software version, archive, and delivery protection controls.
evidence.evidenceText: Clauses 5.8.1-5.8.8 reference Software Validation Report A-SVR-CYN Rev.0 for verification completion, unresolved anomalies, revision history, environment, archiving, and delivery without corruption or unauthorised change, with verdict P.

evidence.claim: Software evidence mapping identifies Rev.0 software validation documents dated 2025-09-25 and software development plan documents dated 2025-05-25.
evidence.evidenceText: Software mapping table lists Software Validation Report A-SVR-CYN Rev.0 deliverables dated 2025-09-25 and Software Development Plan A-SDP-CYN Rev.0 deliverables dated 2025-05-25.

evidence.claim: Annex A is an IEC 60601-1-6 usability test report issued under CB Scheme on 2025-11-11.
evidence.evidenceText: Annex A title is IEC 60601-1-6 Test Report; report number ACTS-2025-SM-261_Annex A; date of issue 2025-11-11; test procedure CB Scheme.

evidence.claim: Annex A says no national or group differences were declared for the EU Group and US, and the product fulfills EN 60601-1-6:2010/A1:2015/A2:2021.
evidence.evidenceText: Summary of compliance states EU Group and US: no National or Group Differences declared; product fulfills EN 60601-1-6:2010/A1:2015/A2:2021.

evidence.claim: Usability file documents reviewed include usability validation, engineering file, formative evaluation, and summative evaluation documents, all Rev.0.
evidence.evidenceText: General product information lists Usability Validation Plan A-UVP-CYN Rev.0, Usability Validation Report A-UVR-CYN Rev.0, Usability Engineering File A-UEF-CYN Rev.0, Formative Evaluation Plan/Report, and Summative Evaluation Plan/Report Rev.0.

evidence.claim: IEC 60601-1-6 usability engineering process was found compliant with IEC 62366-1:2015+A1:2020.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 4.2 states usability engineering process complies with IEC 62366-1 including amended definitions; inspection verified manufacturer demonstrated compliance with IEC 62366-1:2015+A1:2020, verdict P.

reviewFlags.reason: Chunk starts mid-report at page 76, so main report cover-page metadata such as original IEC 60601-1 issue date and full model list may be in another chunk.
Source text is OCR/PDF encoded with mojibake characters; extracted facts rely on readable reconstructed content and should be cross-checked against the original PDF for final regulatory use.
Annex A has its own report number suffix and issue date; documentDate uses the Annex A date supported in this chunk.
